title: Mutton Rogan Josh

ingredients.title: For the Mutton
items: Mutton (goat/lamb, bone-in preferred) – 1 kg, medium-sized pieces
Mustard oil – 120 ml (or ghee for a richer restaurant version)

ingredients.title: For Creaminess & Balance
items: Onion paste – 2 medium onions, browned and blended (optional)

ingredients.title: For Finishing
items: Warm water or mutton stock – 600–700 ml
Fresh coriander leaves – small handful (for garnish)
Saffron strands – 8–10, soaked in 2 tbsp warm milk (optional)

instruction.title: Prepare the Mutton
steps: Wash and pat dry the mutton pieces.
Heat mustard oil until it reaches its smoking point, then reduce the heat.
Fry the mutton pieces in batches until browned and sealed on all sides.
Remove and set aside.

instruction.title: Infuse the Spices
steps: In the same oil, add green cardamoms, black cardamoms, cloves, cinnamon stick, and bay leaves.
Cook until fragrant.
Add asafoetida (hing).
Mix Kashmiri red chili powder with 2 tbsp warm water and stir it into the oil to prevent burning.

instruction.title: Build the Curry Base
steps: Return the browned mutton to the pan and coat it well in the spiced oil.
Add fennel powder, dry ginger powder, coriander powder, turmeric, and salt.
Reduce the heat to low.
Gradually add the whisked yogurt while stirring continuously to prevent curdling.
If using, stir in the browned onion paste for a richer and silkier gravy.

instruction.title: Slow Cook
steps: Pour in the hot water or mutton stock.
Cover and simmer on low heat for 60–90 minutes until the mutton becomes tender.
Stir occasionally during cooking.
Adjust the gravy consistency so it is medium-thick and coats the meat well.

instruction.title: Finish the Dish
steps: Stir in the garam masala.
Add the saffron-infused milk if using.
Simmer for another 5 minutes.
Allow the curry to rest for 15 minutes before serving for the best flavor.

instruction.title: Serving Suggestions
steps: Serve hot with steamed basmati rice.
Pair with naan, sheermal, or saffron pulao for a restaurant-style meal.
Garnish lightly with fresh coriander leaves.
Avoid adding heavy cream to maintain the authentic Kashmiri flavor.

chefTips: Use bone-in mutton for a richer and more flavorful curry.
Slow cooking is essential for tender meat and deep flavor development.
Kashmiri red chili powder provides the signature vibrant color without excessive heat.
Add yogurt gradually over low heat while stirring continuously to prevent splitting.
The curry is perfectly cooked when the oil separates and floats on top of the gravy.
